$comment: Normative appendix for L1-HO-01; extracted from hooks/task_lifecycle_gate.py handoff required-fields at plugin 4.6.8 / commit b4041ccf. The as-built gate lists reasoning_chain among its checked fields at advisory severity; this schema makes it optional per the ratified prose canon, and conformance annexes record the stricter gate as satisfied-with-deviation.
title: HANDOFF payload
description: A Specialist's structured completion claim, recorded in the work record's metadata. Shape-only: recording preconditions and lifecycle semantics live in the prose requirements L1-HO-01 through L1-HO-03.

produced.description: Deliverables created or modified, with locations.

decisions.description: Decisions taken, each with rationale; assumptions that could be wrong.

reasoning_chain.description: How the key decisions connect. Optional in shape; recommended for non-trivial work per L1-HO-01.

oneOf.description: Prioritized entries: every item carries a priority prefix.
Empty uncertainty set: exactly the no-uncertainty sentence, alone.

uncertainty.description: Prioritized uncertainty entries. Each entry is prefixed with its priority from the closed set HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW. An empty uncertainty set is declared with the explicit no-uncertainty sentence as the array's only element — never by omitting the field, never by an empty array, and never mixed with prioritized entries.

integration.description: Other components touched or depended on.

open_questions.description: Unresolved items for the Lead or downstream actors.

produced: services/payments/api: refund endpoint wired to worker
decisions: Idempotency key derived from charge id + amount — retries must not double-refund
examples.reasoning_chain: Refunds are retried by the queue on timeout, so the worker had to be idempotent, which required a deterministic idempotency key.
uncertainty: [HIGH] Partial-refund rounding for multi-currency charges — verify against ledger fixtures
open_questions: Should refunds older than 90 days route to manual review?
